summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a Rust chat service, then handle a new P1 issue found by a Greptile automated review.
summary.outcome: All four issues resolved (3 original PR comments + 1 new Greptile P1), committed, pushed, and PR threads resolved.

repo: Config should be read from the session's state snapshot (`state.config`) rather than re-reading from disk — this pattern applies in both summary.rs and send.rs.
Greptile is used as an automated PR reviewer; its check results are polled via GitHub check runs API and inline comments fetched separately.
The project uses a worktree-based development flow under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/honeysuckle-sycamore/.
YAML frontmatter values must be wrapped in double-quotes with escaping of `\` and `"` characters via a `yaml_quote()` helper.
Streaming-only providers lack tool support, so periodic extraction must be gated on a `stream_only` guard.

code.finding: Config values must be captured before `persona` is moved into `agent_fut`; use the session's config snapshot instead of calling `discover_and_load()` at the point of use.

workflow: Greptile review response format can change between polling attempts, causing jq parsing errors — handle by checking the endpoint directly rather than relying on parsed poll output.
After pushing a fix, both committing/pushing and resolving GitHub PR threads are part of the same workflow step.
Format, check, and lint are run as a single combined step before committing.

friction: Greptile check polling produced jq parsing errors due to a response format change after review completion, requiring a direct endpoint check as a workaround.
Confidence score required multiple bash attempts (PR body, check run title, check run summary) before being located.
